other_observers: None; I was the original finder. Dan Maynard and several birders from the West Slope.
specific_location: At the east end of Blue Mesa Reservoir
description: I spotted this loon while driving by the area and the first thing that came to my mind was that it was holding its bill in an upward angle, so my first thought was this is probably either a Red-throated or Yellow-billed. While watching the bird when I stopped it would usually hold its bill upward other than times when it would put its face in the water presumably looking for food, which was quite often. The first thing I noticed was how light the face was and that the outer half of the bill appeared yellow. The yellow bill was very large and boat shaped, meaning flat on the top and very curved on the lower mandible. The cheek is very white with the white going up to or just past the eye. It has a brown back of the neck going all the way up the top of the head. It was darkest where the top of the bill meets the head and lightest on the sides of the neck. I was able to see a hint of an ear patch on the bird. The overall body coloring was fairly light brown, at least light compared to other loons. It also had white lines going across the back of the body. The overall size was a very long, large and bulky bird.Behaviors: Swimming and diving in the reservoirCall: Didn’t hear itPlumage: First Summer
